idea: X-ray any git repo into an interactive HTML visualization — hotspots, treemap, code age, author territories
takeaway: Pure stdlib Python + single HTML output is a sweet spot — zero deps, instant sharing

idea: Visualize all running processes as an interactive force-directed graph — node size=RAM, color=CPU, clickable tree branches
takeaway: Canvas-based force simulation handles 380+ nodes fine with stdlib-only ps parsing

idea: Terminal typing speed test using real code snippets from your own repos — curses TUI with live WPM and history tracking
takeaway: Curses is underrated for quick TUIs — scanning for real code snippets makes practice actually relevant

idea: WiFi monitoring web app with timeline scrubber — signal charts, network heatmap, browse any moment in history
takeaway: nmcli -t gives perfectly structured WiFi data; JSONL + polling API = instant time-series UI with zero deps

idea: Live regex tester with match highlighting, capture group extraction, and quick reference cheat sheet
takeaway: JS RegExp + innerHTML replacement is all you need for a full-featured regex tool

idea: Paste any JSON, explore as collapsible tree with search, path copying, format/minify
takeaway: Recursive HTML generation with random IDs makes collapsible trees trivial

idea: Paste a cron expression, see human-readable description, next 10 runs, and weekly fire heatmap
takeaway: Cron parsing is just 5 field parsers + a date iterator — the heatmap sells it

idea: Interactive color palette generator with HSL sliders, contrast checker, harmonies, and CSS export
takeaway: HSL is the right color space for UI tools — everything (shades, harmonies) is just hue rotation

idea: Write markdown, get an instant slide deck with live preview, thumbnails, and fullscreen presentation mode
takeaway: A minimal markdown parser (~20 regexes) is enough for 90% of presentation content

idea: Define mock API endpoints in a browser UI, get a running server with request logging and path params
takeaway: Python's http.server + a single HTML page is a complete mock API platform

idea: Paste two text blocks, see beautiful side-by-side or unified diff with character-level highlighting
takeaway: LCS-based diff with char-level second pass gives GitHub-quality diffs in ~60 lines of JS

idea: Browser synthesizer with keyboard input, waveform viz, reverb, and ADSR envelope controls
takeaway: Web Audio API oscillators + convolver reverb = surprisingly capable synth in one HTML file

idea: GitHub-style contribution grid for tracking daily habits with streaks, colors, and localStorage persistence
takeaway: localStorage + a 52x7 grid is all you need for a habit tracker that actually sticks

idea: Scan any directory and visualize disk usage as an interactive drillable treemap with file type coloring
takeaway: Recursive slice-and-dice layout is simple and fast enough for thousands of files

idea: Visual pomodoro timer with ring animation, session tracking, daily stats, and audio alerts
takeaway: CSS ring + setInterval + localStorage = full productivity timer in one file

idea: Pixel art editor with pen, eraser, fill, line, rect, circle, undo, and PNG export
takeaway: Bresenham line algorithm + flood fill = complete drawing tools in vanilla JS

idea: Interactive function plotter with pan, zoom, multiple functions, and presets
takeaway: new Function() for safe math eval + canvas = full graphing calculator

idea: Drag-and-drop task board with editable cards, columns, tags, and persistence
takeaway: HTML5 drag-and-drop API is clunky but works well enough for a kanban board

idea: Fullscreen drawing canvas with pen, shapes, text, eraser, undo, and PNG export
takeaway: Canvas imageData snapshots make undo trivial for drawing apps

idea: Searchable emoji picker with categories, recent history, and click-to-copy
takeaway: A curated 120-emoji dataset with keywords beats scraping Unicode tables
cull_reason: Phase 3 usefulness: built into modern OS

idea: Live system resource monitor — CPU, memory, disk, network, top processes in browser
takeaway: /proc filesystem has everything you need — no psutil dependency required

idea: QR code generator with custom colors, error correction, presets, and PNG/SVG export
takeaway: QR encoding from scratch is doable in ~150 lines — GF(256) math is the hard part

idea: Universal unit converter — length, weight, temp, time, data, speed, area, volume, pressure
takeaway: Unit conversion is just a lookup table of ratios to a base unit — temperature is the only special case

idea: Multiple named countdown timers with progress bars, quick presets, and persistence
takeaway: setInterval(render, 1000) + Date.now() arithmetic = instant countdown wall

idea: Speed dial new tab page with clock, search, grouped bookmarks, and localStorage
takeaway: A new tab replacement is the perfect single-HTML project — you use it every day

idea: Wolfenstein 3D-style raycasting engine with DDA, procedural textures, map editor, and head bobbing
takeaway: DDA + direct ImageData pixel buffer + bitwise ops = smooth 60fps raycaster in vanilla JS

idea: Interactive Mandelbrot/Julia set explorer with smooth coloring, zoom-to-cursor, progressive rendering, and 6 palettes
takeaway: Uint32Array pixel writes + progressive rendering during interaction = smooth fractal explorer
cull_reason: Phase 3 usefulness: zoom once, never return

idea: Real-time audio visualizer with 5 modes — bars, waveform, circular spectrum, particle burst, mountain range
takeaway: Web Audio API AnalyserNode + Canvas = instant music visualizer with zero deps

idea: N-body gravity simulator with merging, trails, zoom/pan, and orbital presets
takeaway: Symplectic Euler + sub-stepping + softening = stable orbits; store trails as arrays for camera-correct rendering

idea: Interactive logic gate circuit simulator with wiring, truth tables, and a half-adder example
takeaway: Iterative evaluation (N+2 passes) handles arbitrary gate ordering without topological sort

idea: Procedural micro-climate simulator with 10 weather states, parallax landscape, lightning, aurora, and smooth transitions
takeaway: Deriving weather from physical inputs (temp+humidity) beats hardcoded states — lerp smoothing makes transitions magical

idea: Musical tower defense where towers play pentatonic synth notes quantized to 120 BPM — your defense becomes a composition
takeaway: Pentatonic scale guarantees harmony no matter how chaotic; DynamicsCompressor prevents clipping with polyphony

idea: RPG where you fight monsters by writing regex patterns — teaches regex from basics to lookaheads through combat
takeaway: Gamifying a learning curve works when difficulty tiers map to skill tiers; ReDoS protection via timed execution is essential for user-input regex

idea: Draw data points, watch a neural network learn to classify them live — backprop from scratch with decision boundary heatmap
takeaway: Xavier init + gradient clipping + L2 reg = stable training in vanilla JS; 40x40 grid with Float32Array keeps boundary rendering fast

idea: Paste text, see Markov chain as force-directed graph, click nodes to steer real-time text generation
takeaway: Force-directed layout + Markov walk = text becomes a playable instrument; log-scale node sizing tames Zipf's law
cull_reason: Phase 3 usefulness: generate once, boring quickly

idea: Classic Asteroids but destroying rocks spawns green ally and red enemy AI drones — the battlefield becomes an ecosystem
takeaway: Familiar mechanics + one emergent twist = instant fun; drone separation forces prevent the clumping blob problem

idea: Drop a CSV file, watch animated bar chart races, line charts, and scatter plots — auto-detected columns, zero config
takeaway: Auto-inference with fallback manual mapping = best of both worlds; lerp factor 0.12 makes bars slide buttery smooth

idea: Single-file REST client — mini Postman with syntax highlighting, CORS proxy, localStorage persistence, and presets
takeaway: localStorage auto-save makes dev tools sticky; CORS proxy toggle with security warning is the right UX balance

idea: Draw walls, race 6 pathfinding algorithms simultaneously on the same grid — A*, Dijkstra, BFS, Greedy, DFS, Bidirectional
takeaway: Layering algorithms on one grid with offset paths beats split-screen; integer key y*COLS+x gives O(1) Set lookups

idea: Classic minesweeper but flagged mines EXPLODE clearing adjacent tiles — chain reactions, hex grid, screen shake
takeaway: Turning a passive action (flagging) into an offensive weapon transforms the entire gameplay loop; hex grid tightens chain balance

idea: Audiovisual generative art — particles follow noise fields, attractors trigger pentatonic synth based on density
takeaway: Float32Array with stride access = zero-GC particle engine; long-exposure fade + additive blending = instant beauty; density-based audio triggers prevent polyphony choking
cull_reason: Phase 3 usefulness: pretty screensaver, used once

idea: Type git commands, watch the commit DAG animate — learn branching, merging, rebasing, cherry-picking visually
takeaway: Mock git engine is just a DAG with branch pointers; BFS ancestor check distinguishes fast-forward from merge commit; rebase = collect non-ancestor commits + replay onto target

idea: Live webcam to real-time ASCII art — 5 character sets (including braille + emoji), themes, snapshot PNG export
takeaway: 3-second time-to-value beats feature depth; downscale before getImageData; array.join beats string concat for GC
cull_reason: Phase 3 usefulness: webcam gimmick used once

idea: Audio-visual reaction tester — rising synth tension builds then drops, measure reflexes in milliseconds with histogram stats
takeaway: Synthesized audio tension (rising sine + LFO noise) creates genuine anxiety before the drop; pointerdown > click for zero-delay mobile input
cull_reason: Phase 3 usefulness: superseded by neon-reflex killed too; redundant pair

idea: Paste text, get instant visual breakdown — Flesch-Kincaid, sentence rhythm, vocabulary richness, top words
takeaway: Gemini code audit found 5 real bugs when given actual code vs summaries; sentence splitting needs abbreviation protection; syllable regex must use + not {1,2}

idea: Roguelike where movement makes pentatonic music, combat is percussion, and low health compresses the audio into tension
takeaway: Gemini code audit found 3 real gameplay bugs (double attack, enemy stacking, multiple game-over calls) when given actual code

idea: Brutalist typing speed test — instant start, live WPM, combo streaks, synthesized key sounds, micro-shake errors, competitive sharing
takeaway: Gemini found invisible bug: wrong chars never rendered red because chars array wasn't mutated on error — must advance cursor AND store wrong char

idea: Drum synth + step sequencer — 4 tracks with shapeable sounds, all built from Web Audio nodes, no samples
takeaway: Gemini found 4 bugs: swing math reversed+drifting, exponentialRamp crashes at zero volume, noise buffer GC pressure, DOM query waste. Cache noise buffers once, clamp volumes to 0.001 minimum.

idea: Mesmerizing pendulum wave physics — adjustable count, spread, damping, with optional pentatonic audio on zero-crossings
takeaway: Gemini caught prevSign hardcode, resize not re-initing, and audio frequency wrapping — all invisible to static analysis

idea: Mobile-first breathing exercise PWA — OLED black, synthesized tones, haptic feedback, Screen Wake Lock, 4 patterns
takeaway: First mobile-first PWA build. Tab-inactive dt must be capped to prevent catch-up storms. WakeLock needs release listener. iOS needs click fallback for audio.

idea: Brutalist mobile dB meter — microphone RMS-to-decibel, color-coded levels, haptic alerts on dangerous noise, wake lock
takeaway: iOS requires AudioContext created synchronously BEFORE async getUserMedia. Vibration API at 60fps = battery death — always throttle. Resume AudioContext on visibilitychange.

idea: Physics-driven spinning wheel for group decisions — haptic ticks, synthesized clicks, dramatic landing, customizable options
takeaway: Use segment index not indexOf for result coloring (handles duplicates). Enforce minimum canvas size to prevent negative arc radius crash.

idea: HIIT/Tabata workout timer — massive countdown, synthesized audio, haptic patterns, absolute-time accuracy, wake lock
takeaway: Use Date.now() absolute timestamps for timers, not dt subtraction — dt=0 cap kills background counting. Schedule audio via audioCtx.currentTime, not setTimeout.

idea: Minimalist daily journal — one line per day, monthly mosaic calendar, streak tracker, localStorage persistence
takeaway: The constraint IS the feature — 80 chars forces distillation like Twitter created a medium. Streak calculation must tolerate 'not yet written today' by checking yesterday.

idea: Fullscreen gradient designer — pick colors, choose type, copy CSS, save favorites. The preview IS the UI.
takeaway: First project using click-only events (no pointerdown). Toast timeouts need clearTimeout to prevent race conditions. Always Array.isArray guard on localStorage parse.

idea: Snake but beautiful — HSL gradient ribbon body, Canvas glow, pentatonic notes on eat, neon light-painting aesthetic
takeaway: Only preventDefault on game-relevant keys. Never DOM-query in 60fps render loop. touchmove must be prevented during mobile gameplay. Always handle win state.

idea: Locally encrypted notepad — AES-256-GCM, PBKDF2 600K iterations, auto-lock on tab switch, zero server
takeaway: Gemini security audit elevated PBKDF2 from 100K to 600K iterations (OWASP). Password min length, beforeunload warning, autocomplete=new-password all critical for security apps.

idea: Camera color eyedropper — point phone at anything, tap to sample hex/RGB/HSL, build palettes from the real world
takeaway: Single getImageData for NxN area is 9x faster than N*N individual calls. Always check navigator.mediaDevices existence before getUserMedia. iOS clipboard needs setSelectionRange.

idea: Type any word, watch a unique procedural tree grow — deterministic generative art from name hash, bezier curves, curated palettes
takeaway: Seeded PRNG (mulberry32) gives infinite deterministic entropy from any string. Sort recursive branches by depth for natural growth animation. Cap branch count to prevent CPU freeze.
cull_reason: Phase 3 usefulness: type once, novelty visualizer

idea: Conway's Game of Life with age-based gradient coloring — newborn cyan, aging purple, ghost trails on death
takeaway: Ghost decay tied to render loop runs during pause — must guard with `if (playing)`. Scope touchmove prevention to canvas, not document. Add pointercancel alongside pointerup/leave.

idea: Procedural maze runner with glowing neon trail, escalating grid sizes, timed runs, swipe-slides-until-wall mobile controls
takeaway: Cache DOM elements queried in rAF loops. Smart trail: pop on backtrack, push on advance. Mobile maze navigation needs slide-until-wall (one swipe = many cells). Show clamped next-level text AFTER incrementing.
cull_reason: Phase 3 usefulness: solve once, no replay

idea: Obsessively polished coin flip — CSS 3D rotation, synthesized audio, haptic patterns, cumulative rotation tracking
takeaway: CSS 3D rotation must track cumulative angle (+=) not absolute (=) to prevent backward spinning. Always guard localStorage numbers with isNaN + Math.max(0). Catch audioCtx.resume() promises.

idea: 16x16 pixel art editor with live tiling preview — paint a pattern, see it repeat as wallpaper, download PNG
takeaway: Never call toDataURL in pointermove — use rAF dirty flag. Use toBlob for large canvas downloads. setPointerCapture prevents stuck drag. touch-action:none on drawing surfaces.

idea: Fullscreen clock with 4 faces (Classic, Bauhaus, Radar, Binary) — smooth sweep, high-DPI, wake lock, tap to cycle
takeaway: createConicGradient needs feature detection fallback. Wake lock is more reliable when triggered by user gesture than on load. Skip render when W/H = 0 (minimized).

idea: Random name picker with slot-machine animation + team splitter — enter names, pick one or split teams
takeaway: Animation state flags must be checked by ALL action functions, not just the one that sets them. Handle edge case of single item in random-pick UIs.

idea: Tip calculator — bill input, preset/custom tip %, split between people, live calculation
takeaway: Clearing a custom input must reset the underlying state variable (ghost value). Use parseFloat not parseInt for numeric inputs that could be decimal.

idea: Classic Sudoku with algorithmic generation, 3 difficulties, rule-based validation (not solution comparison)
takeaway: Never validate puzzles against ONE stored solution — use the game's RULES to validate. Random cell removal can create multiple valid solutions.

idea: Tic Tac Toe vs minimax AI — unbeatable hard mode, easy random mode, click spam prevention, score tracking
takeaway: Any setTimeout-delayed AI response creates a click-spam window. Must lock input with a flag during the delay, reset on AI completion and new game.

idea: Spaced repetition flashcard app with 3D card flip animation and adaptive review scheduling
takeaway: Gemini caught that parseEditor wiped all study progress on every edit — preserve progress by matching existing cards

idea: Falling sand physics sandbox with cellular automata — 7 elements with density displacement and emergent chemistry
takeaway: Bit-flag bitmask (high bit of Uint8Array) elegantly prevents double-processing of upward-moving particles without needing a separate array

idea: Brutalist 64-bit binary explorer with IEEE 754 visualization, endianness toggle, and text/number injection
takeaway: toPrecision regex can destroy integers — Number(toPrecision()).toString() is safe. DataView endianness must be a flag, not a byte reversal.

idea: Radial symmetry drawing tool with neon glow, auto-cycling colors, and ephemeral fading trails
takeaway: High-DPI canvas must set BOTH canvas.width (buffer) AND canvas.style.width (CSS layout) — missing CSS dims causes overflow on Retina screens
cull_reason: Phase 3 usefulness: draw once, novelty toy

idea: Pixel sorting and chromatic aberration tool for generative glitch art with drag-and-drop image input
takeaway: UI state must always match visual state — never apply effect with temp values then reset sliders. Threshold sliders need cross-clamping.

idea: Brutalist Morse code trainer with Koch method progression and Farnsworth timing
takeaway: Rolling accuracy history must reset on level-up to prevent cascading progression. Always wire up helper functions — dead code means missing features.

idea: Dice notation parser with discrete probability histogram, crypto RNG, and percentile overlay
takeaway: Never sample rolls from a precomputed distribution — always simulate the actual dice independently. for...in on objects is vulnerable to prototype pollution; use Object.keys.

idea: Generative ambient engine with Euclidean polyrhythms, FM synthesis, and algorithmic delay reverb
takeaway: Complex recursive algorithms (Bjorklund) can silently produce empty outputs — simpler Bresenham/modulo approach is more reliable. Lookahead audio scheduling causes premature visual flash — must clamp negative elapsed.

idea: Connect Four with minimax AI, alpha-beta pruning, 3 difficulty levels, and center-weighted heuristic
takeaway: renderBoard must not wipe all CSS classes — only toggle player classes to preserve animations and state classes. Minimax needs depth factor in terminal scores to avoid stalling.
cull_reason: Phase 3 usefulness: too basic, boring quickly

idea: Cryptographically secure password/passphrase generator with entropy visualization and scramble animation
takeaway: Settings changes must regenerate output — showing stale data with updated metrics is a UX desync. cancelAnimationFrame prevents scramble animation race conditions.

idea: 5-letter word deduction game with two-pass duplicate letter evaluation and virtual keyboard
takeaway: Two-pass evaluation (exact matches first, then partials against remaining counts) is essential for correct Wordle-style duplicate letter handling. Only update the active DOM row on keystroke, not the entire grid.

idea: Brutalist 4-track 16-step drum sequencer with synthesized Web Audio and visual queue sync
takeaway: Swing math must be symmetric (add X to even, subtract X from odd) or tempo drifts. Visual events must be queued and triggered at audioCtx.currentTime, not at schedule time.

idea: 2048-style sliding puzzle themed as stellar nucleosynthesis — merge elements from Hydrogen to Iron
takeaway: Game over detection must respect merge ceiling — if max-tier tiles cant merge, canMove must skip them or game softlocks. Win state alerts need a one-time flag.

idea: Brutalist dual-timer chess clock with 180° split screen, Fischer increment, and Wake Lock
takeaway: Chess convention: tapping starts OPPONENT clock, not yours. Background tabs cause rAF throttling — must auto-pause and reset lastTick. WakeLock needs guard against orphaned locks.

idea: Neon breakout/brick breaker with paddle-relative bounce, particle effects, and ball trailing
takeaway: Brick collision must force velocity AWAY from brick center, not just invert — blind inversion causes trapping. Keyboard input needs held-key map, not key repeat reliance.

idea: Color harmony generator with HSL wheel, 5 harmony modes, click-to-copy, and CSS variable export
takeaway: Clipboard writeText is async — toast must go in .then(), not after the call. Hex input should accept 3-char shorthand and optional # for better UX.

idea: Polyphonic synthesizer keyboard with QWERTY mapping, 4 waveforms, octave shift, and delay effect
takeaway: exponentialRampToValueAtTime crashes when gain is 0 — use setTargetAtTime for safe zero-approach. Window blur must stopAll notes to prevent stuck keys on tab switch.

idea: Falling block puzzle (Tetris) with 7-bag randomizer, ghost piece, wall kicks, and neon glow
takeaway: O-piece must skip rotation entirely (it is rotationally symmetric, wall kicks shift it). Game over needs restart delay so player sees final board state.

idea: Developer base converter with BigInt, live bidirectional sync, and auto-formatted binary/decimal
takeaway: Input formatting (commas, spaces) destroys cursor position on reassignment. Must track cursor relative to data characters, then restore after formatting.

idea: Visual reaction time test — 5 rounds with false start penalties, grade ranking, and all-time best
takeaway: Key auto-repeat (e.repeat) must be guarded in any single-tap game. Double-fire from multiple input sources needs cooldown. Play-again flow should not require extra taps.
cull_reason: Phase 3 usefulness: test once, never return

idea: Typography sandbox with 13 fonts, variable weight, CSS export, and dark/light toggle
takeaway: Never use || for numeric fallbacks — 0 is falsy in JS. Use isNaN() check instead. CSS variables are the cleanest way to drive live typography previews.

idea: Pixel art animation studio with frame timeline, onion skinning, flood fill, and sprite sheet export
takeaway: Never rebuild DOM canvas elements on every pixel paint — update only the active thumbnail. Dead conditional branches (checking impossible tool state) should be simplified.

idea: Sorting algorithm visualizer with 4 algorithms, animated bars, audio feedback, and async cancellation
takeaway: Async sorting algorithms need cancel checks at EVERY loop level — outer for loops, between recursive branches, inside merge loops. Audio nodes need onended disconnect.

idea: Canvas drawing app with stroke-based undo/redo, bezier smoothing, baked history, and PNG export
takeaway: History cap (shift) deletes data from the source-of-truth array, causing redrawn canvas to lose strokes. Bake oldest strokes to an offscreen canvas before removing. pointercancel should discard, not save.

idea: Zero-dependency markdown previewer with custom regex parser, sync scroll, and localStorage persistence
takeaway: Custom markdown parser must: 1) escape HTML first (XSS), 2) extract code blocks before inline parsing, 3) process block elements before inline. Regex-based brace balance tests are unreliable for regex-heavy code.
cull_reason: Phase 3 usefulness: superseded by modern IDEs

idea: Memory card matching game with CSS 3D flips, multiple difficulties, and timer
takeaway: Pending setTimeout chains must be tracked and cleared on game reset — otherwise they fire on stale/rewritten state arrays and crash or corrupt the new game.

idea: Classic Pong vs AI with neon glow, ball trail, screen shake, and increasing speed
takeaway: Paddle collision must snap ball to edge (not push by fixed offset) to prevent tunneling at high speed. Keyboard game controls must use held-key map, not targetY offsets.

idea: Hangman-style word guessing with hacker theme, trace bar, and tech word list
takeaway: Never use el.className = value to set state classes — it wipes all existing classes. Always use classList.add/remove. Object.keys > for...in for object iteration.

idea: Configurable Lorem Ipsum generator with word/sentence/paragraph modes and one-click copy
takeaway: Clipboard API catch must fall back to execCommand, not silently swallow. Dont generate data you immediately overwrite — insert the fixed value directly.

idea: Compound interest savings calculator with inflation adjustment and CSS bar chart
takeaway: When displaying inflation-adjusted totals, ALL components (contributions, interest, total) must be adjusted consistently. Mixing nominal and real values makes the breakdown not add up.

idea: Emergent artificial life — thousands of particles with attraction/repulsion matrix creating organic cells, worms, and ecosystems
takeaway: Particle Life creates stunning emergent behavior from simple rules. Performance: batch render by color, fillRect over arc, pre-calc constants, Float32Array SoA layout. Toroidal wrap prevents edge clumping.

idea: Complete platformer: AABB collision, coyote time, jump buffer, variable jump, parallax, particles, 3 levels, synthesized audio — the game we dodged for 99 builds
takeaway: THE GRAND FINALE. Platformer physics: resolve X then Y separately, use previous position (not velocity sign) for collision direction, cap variable jump once (dont continuously dampen), coyote time + jump buffer are essential for feel.

idea: Interactive cloth simulation — Verlet integration, tear mechanics, tension coloring, wind, drag and toss physics
takeaway: Verlet cloth: pinned points must remember basePinned state for drag/release. Constraint relaxation must weight by pin status (free point takes full correction). Tension rendering needs div-by-zero guard.

idea: Procedural terrain generator with Simplex noise, 13 biomes, hillshading, and real-time parameter sliders
takeaway: Multiplicative PRNGs (Lehmer) produce constant 0 from seed 0 — always clamp seed to min 1. Hillshading near water must clamp neighbor elevations to seaLevel to prevent harsh coastline artifacts.

idea: Boid flocking simulation with separation/alignment/cohesion, 4 color groups, predator mode, and oriented triangles
takeaway: Boid updates must be two-pass (calc all forces, then apply) to prevent directional drift from sequential in-place updates. Mouse position sentinel (-9999) wraps to visible via toroidal math — guard with mouseX > -1.

idea: ASCII roguelike dungeon crawler with procedural rooms, FOV, turn-based combat, enemy AI, and infinite descent
takeaway: FOV and draw functions must bounds-check array access (visible[y] can be undefined for camera offsets). CSS comments with keyword overlay trigger test false positives.

idea: Draw a waveform shape, DFT converts it to PeriodicWave, play it on a keyboard synthesizer with envelope and delay
takeaway: initAudio() must be called BEFORE checking audioCtx in playNote. Use osc.stop(time) on audio thread instead of setTimeout for cleanup. onended with reference comparison prevents rapid-retrigger cleanup bugs.

idea: Interactive force-directed graph visualizer — add nodes, draw edges, self-organizing physics with Coulomb repulsion and Hooke springs
takeaway: Overlapping nodes (dx=dy=0) produce zero force even with distance clamping — must add random jitter to separate. Terminal velocity cap prevents explosion when repulsion is high.

idea: Image dithering tool with Floyd-Steinberg, Atkinson, Bayer algorithms and retro color palettes
takeaway: Error diffusion MUST use Float32Array buffer, not Uint8ClampedArray — clamped arrays truncate accumulated error causing banding. Diffuse into float buffer, write final to uint8 output.

idea: Interactive CSS cubic-bezier easing editor with draggable handles, animation preview, presets, and one-click CSS copy
takeaway: SVG is ideal for interactive curve editors — crisp vector rendering, native draggable elements, easy path commands. Y-axis must allow values outside 0-1 for overshoot/bounce effects while X stays clamped to 0-1 per CSS spec.

idea: Pure software 3D wireframe renderer with 7 polyhedra, rotation matrices, perspective projection, z-depth coloring — no WebGL
takeaway: Depth coloring must map t=1 to NEAR (bright) not far. Painters algorithm must sort DESCENDING Z (far first). Wrap rotation angles with modulo 2pi to prevent precision loss.

idea: Tower defense with 3 tower types, wave economy, splash damage, path-following enemies, and dt-based projectile physics
takeaway: Projectile movement and lifetime MUST use dt (frame-rate independent). Towers must skip dead enemies in targeting. Zero-distance targeting causes NaN velocity — clamp distance.

idea: Rhythm-action typing game — type words to the beat with procedural drums, audio-synced falling notes, timing scoring, and combos
takeaway: Rhythm game note positions MUST be anchored to audioCtx.currentTime, not dt-based falling. Fall speed must be locked per-note at spawn or speed changes desync in-flight notes. Missed vs completed notes need distinct visual behavior.

idea: Maze algorithm visualizer — animated generation (Backtracker/Prim/Kruskal) and solving (BFS/DFS) with step-by-step mode
takeaway: Union-Find for Kruskals needs tree-based union (link root to root) not O(N) flat scan. Path compression in findSet + tree linking in unionSets gives near O(1) amortized.

idea: Photo to ASCII art converter with 5 character sets, color mode, brightness/contrast, and PNG export
takeaway: Character sets containing HTML entities (<, >, &) MUST be escaped when inserted via innerHTML. Character index mapping should use lum/256*length (not lum/255*(length-1)) for even distribution.

idea: Physics playground — draw ramps, drop balls and boxes, grab and throw objects with custom collision engine
takeaway: Circle-line collision: project center onto segment, find closest point, push out along normal, reflect velocity. Custom physics engines are viable for simple interactions without external libs.

idea: Generalized cellular automata sandbox with 6 rulesets, custom B/S parser, 3-state Brian Brain, cell aging heatmap, and pattern library
takeaway: Multi-state CA engines need flexible state handling (Brians Brain uses 3 states not 2). Cell aging via Uint16Array creates stunning heatmaps. Double-buffered grid swap is essential. Finally built the CA sandbox after dodging it for 20+ builds.

idea: Interactive Voronoi diagram — click to add seeds, drag to reshape, brute-force pixel rendering with edge detection and 4 color modes
takeaway: Brute-force Voronoi (nearest seed per pixel) is simple to implement, visually stunning, and performant at 2x downscale. Edge detection via distance differential between 1st and 2nd nearest seed creates clean cell boundaries.

idea: Interactive music theory — Circle of Fifths, diatonic chords, 8-slot progression builder with synth playback and preset progressions
takeaway: Music theory tools need creation, not just reference. A progression builder that PLAYS chords engages for minutes vs a static chart for seconds. Strum delay (40ms between tones) makes synth chords sound musical.

idea: Interactive fiction engine with branching sci-fi survival story, inventory, stats, conditional choices, and 4 endings
takeaway: Interactive fiction engines need: JSON node graph, action system (items/stats), conditional choices, and a compelling pre-loaded story. Typography-focused UI with serif font + high line-height creates immersive reading experience.

idea: L-System fractal explorer with 6 presets, custom rules, auto-fit bounding box, gradient coloring, and turtle graphics
takeaway: L-systems need auto-fit: dry-run turtle path to get bounding box, then scale/translate to center in viewport. String length cap (500K) prevents exponential growth from crashing browser. The project we dodged for 20+ builds — finally shipped.

idea: Interactive spirograph — hypotrochoid/epitrochoid curves with animated drawing, gradient coloring, gear visualization, and 6 presets
takeaway: Spirograph loop closure: calculate rotations needed via GCD(R,r), then maxTheta = (r/gcd)*2PI. Parametric curves need per-segment coloring for gradient effect. Gear visualization makes the math tangible.

idea: Oscilloscope-style Lissajous curve explorer with phosphor trails, glow effect, auto-phase, and 4 color modes
takeaway: Phosphor trail effect: dont clearRect, instead fillRect with rgba(0,0,0,alpha) each frame. shadowBlur + dark background = convincing oscilloscope glow.

idea: Extract dominant colors from images using k-means clustering — upload photo, get palette with hex codes and CSS variables
takeaway: K-means on sampled pixel grid (100x100) is fast enough for real-time palette extraction. Sort centroids by luminance for visually pleasing output.

idea: Double pendulum with chaotic motion, trail traces, and dual butterfly-effect mode showing divergence from tiny initial difference
takeaway: Double pendulum Lagrangian equations produce genuinely chaotic motion. Dual mode (0.001 radian difference) visually demonstrates butterfly effect better than any textbook.

idea: Typing speed race against AI ghost with progress bars, live WPM/accuracy, and selectable ghost difficulty
takeaway: Racing against a visible opponent (ghost progress bar) is far more engaging than solo typing tests. Character-by-character validation with immediate visual feedback keeps accuracy pressure high.

idea: Gravity well particle sandbox — click to place mass points, watch particles orbit, spiral, and get absorbed with speed-based coloring
takeaway: Gemini caught burst button bypassing MAX_P limit and mobile hover opacity issue. Click-to-remove wells is more intuitive than right-click-only on mobile.

idea: Vanilla JS neural network playground — paint data points, watch decision boundary form in real-time with live network diagram
takeaway: Unrolled backprop (no matrix class) is fast and readable. Gemini caught stale loss display (lifetime avg vs current epoch), drag point explosion, and canvas coordinate offset. Low-res ImageData heatmap scaled up is the right pattern for real-time boundary viz.

idea: Navier-Stokes fluid simulation with typography as obstacles — dye bleeds from letters and swirls when you inject velocity
takeaway: Gemini caught critical memory leak: creating offscreen canvas every frame (60/sec) would crash browser. Always pre-allocate offscreen canvases. Normalize mouse deltas by screen size for consistent physics across resolutions.

idea: Interactive sound wave synthesizer with oscilloscope, spectrum analyzer, and 8 guided lessons on sound fundamentals
takeaway: Gemini caught music theory error (lesson set 330Hz as octave instead of 440Hz), audio clicking from destroying all oscillators on type change (fix: set type in-place), and missing actx.resume() for iOS. setTargetAtTime prevents zipper noise on slider changes.

idea: Nonogram logic puzzle game — 15 handcrafted puzzles (5x5 to 15x15), pixel art reveals, 3-strike system, drag-to-fill
takeaway: Gemini caught missing game state lock (play after win/loss), broken drawMode for drag consistency, setTimeout race on retry, and localStorage crash in private browsing. Proper game state machine (playing/won/lost) is essential for any game with win/loss conditions.

idea: Isometric city builder with strategy mechanics — procedural 3D buildings, adjacency bonuses, population/revenue economy
takeaway: Gemini caught infinite money exploit (gameTick on every build gave free revenue), broken z-sorting (nested loops != depth order), and floating text not anchored to world coords. Separate stat updates from revenue generation to prevent exploits.

idea: WCAG color accessibility checker with color blindness simulation, auto-fix, and palette contrast matrix
takeaway: Gemini caught hex regex accepting 4-5 char hex codes (only 3 or 6 are valid), auto-fix testing lightness 5/95 instead of 0/100, and integer rounding dropping contrast below threshold. Strict regex alternation (#xxx|#xxxxxx) is safer than range {3,6}.

idea: 16-step drum machine with procedurally synthesized sounds, lookahead scheduling, swing, and URL-encoded pattern sharing
takeaway: Gemini caught float buffer sizes crashing Web Audio (sampleRate*0.15 can be non-integer), ghost highlight timeouts after stop, and missing swing in URL state. Math.floor all buffer sizes. Always clear pending timeouts on state transitions.

idea: Klondike Solitaire with CSS-rendered cards, smart-tap auto-move, undo, responsive sizing, and best time tracking
takeaway: Gemini caught teleporting card bug (smartTap validated middle card but pop() grabbed top card), getComputedStyle in render loop causing 50+ reflows per interaction, and undo not resetting gameWon flag. Always verify the card being moved matches the card that was validated.

idea: Pixel animation editor with frame timeline, onion skinning, playback preview, and PNG spritesheet export
takeaway: Gemini caught playback crash on delete/resize (out-of-bounds frame index), preview not updating during drawing, FPS change restarting animation from frame 0, and unnecessary timeline DOM rebuild on every pointerup. Module-scoped playback frame index with bounds check prevents crashes.

idea: Fourier Drawing Machine — draw any shape, DFT decomposes into spinning epicycles that reconstruct the drawing
takeaway: Gemini caught numCircles permanent downgrade (global clobbered on small drawings), speed-0 tracePath memory leak (infinite array growth), preset setTimeout race condition, and canvas coordinate offset. drawFrame already uses Math.min — don't also clobber the global.

idea: Digital logic gate simulator — place AND/OR/NOT/XOR/NAND gates, wire them, toggle switches, see signals propagate with glowing wires
takeaway: Gemini caught node stacking (place on existing node creates hidden duplicate), hitTestPin z-order mismatch (forward vs backward iteration), and hardcoded 3-pass simulation limit (breaks deep circuits). Convergence-based simulation with max passes handles both deep circuits and oscillators.

idea: Physics puzzle game — slingshot-aim a ball through gravity wells and repulsors to reach a target, 10 levels with par scoring
takeaway: Gemini caught par=0 labeled as 'Under par' (should be 'Par'), trajectory prediction breaking on repel wells (mismatch with actual physics), resize wiping game progress, and divide-by-zero at well center. Separate resize repositioning from level reset.

idea: Interactive function grapher — type math expressions, see them plotted on zoomable/pannable coordinate plane with dynamic grid
takeaway: Gemini caught canvas aspect ratio distortion (internal height != CSS height), try/catch in tight render loop killing performance, and log() defaulting to natural instead of base-10. Canvas internal dimensions must match CSS dimensions exactly.

idea: Visual cipher explorer — Caesar/Vigenere/XOR with step-by-step character transformation showing the underlying math
takeaway: Gemini caught case destruction in Caesar/Vigenere (toUpperCase before processing lost original casing), innerHTML += in loops causing layout thrashing, and play always resetting to step 0 instead of resuming. Cipher functions must preserve input casing by detecting upper/lower before math.

idea: Color palette generator with 5 harmony modes (analogous/complementary/triadic/split/mono), WCAG text contrast, lock colors, export CSS
takeaway: Gemini caught WCAG luminance threshold at 0.4 (way too high, correct is 0.179), clipboard showing 'Copied!' even on failure, and locked colors breaking harmony when seed changes. Lock should use the locked color AS the seed.

idea: Card-flip memory game with CSS 3D animations, emoji icons, 3 difficulty levels, move/time tracking, best score
takeaway: Gemini caught ghost timeout race condition — switching difficulty/new game while eval timeout pending corrupts new game state. Must clearTimeout(evalTimeout) in newGame(). Also moved card sizing from JS to CSS aspect-ratio for resize resilience.

idea: Sorting algorithm visualizer — 5 algorithms with animated bars, color-coded states, audio tones, real-time stats
takeaway: Gemini caught merge sort visualization snapping (rendered only after merge, not per-write), stats not updating live (updateStats only called at end), and size slider DOM thrashing (recreating 100+ nodes on every pixel drag). Async algorithms with per-step rendering need render+delay at every array write, not just at chunk boundaries.

idea: Pomodoro focus timer with SVG progress ring, timestamp-based timing, synthesized chime, wake lock, session tracking
takeaway: Gemini caught critical progress ring bug — totalDuration clobbered on pause caused ring to jump to 100% on resume. Must separate timeRemaining from totalDuration. Also caught DOM-as-source-of-truth anti-pattern and wake lock not re-acquired on tab return.

idea: Multi-layer CSS box-shadow generator with live preview, presets (soft/hard/neumorphism/glow), customizable box, one-click CSS copy
takeaway: Gemini caught inset toggle rebuilding entire DOM (losing keyboard focus), hex shorthand not expanded (3-char hex produces wrong colors), and clipboard API silent failure on HTTP. Update specific DOM elements instead of rebuilding on minor state changes.

idea: Canvas platformer with variable-height jump, coyote time, AABB collision, 5 hand-crafted levels, coins, spikes, touch controls
takeaway: Gemini caught frozen death particles (updateParticles only ran in playing state), center-point-only spike collision (should be AABB), and no horizontal boundary clamping (player walks into void). Particles must always update regardless of game state.

idea: Generative haiku poetry — 5-7-5 syllable structure, POS+syllable word banks, syntax templates, seasonal themes, zen typography
takeaway: Revived: 40+ theme keywords with word associations, massive dictionary expansion (500+ words per POS), theme input field, fixed seasonal bias bug

idea: Live regex tester with match highlighting, capture groups (numbered+named), flag toggles, replacement preview, cheat sheet
takeaway: Gemini caught falsy empty-string check preventing ^$ matches, invisible zero-width matches, missing named capture groups, and incomplete XSS escaping (missing single quote). text==null instead of !text to allow empty string matching.

idea: JSON formatter and collapsible tree viewer with syntax highlighting, path-on-click, format/minify/copy
takeaway: Gemini caught dead code (impossible null&&!null condition), naive regex syntax highlighting matching inside string values, and dot-notation path generation failing on keys with special characters. Single comprehensive regex that tokenizes JSON properly is the correct approach for syntax highlighting.

idea: Interactive cloth physics with Verlet integration, tearing, pinning, wind, and image texture mapping
takeaway: Gemini caught framerate-dependent physics (dt unused), spongy anchor constraints (0.5 hardcode ignores pinned weight), preset state pollution (flag wind persists), ghost cursor, missing ceiling bounds

idea: Software 3D renderer from scratch — hand-rolled Vec3/Mat4 math, perspective projection, flat shading, z-sorting, orbit camera, no WebGL
takeaway: Gemini caught w-clipping bug (abs(w) let behind-camera verts project), DOM stats every frame (moved to 500ms), and semantic m4transformDir on positions (compute local normal first). Capstone #150.

idea: Zero-dep browser-based ASCII/text diagram renderer — boxes, arrows, auto-layout from simple [Node] --> [Node] syntax — FEEDER for Markdown Deck
takeaway: Bellman-Ford longest-path layering handles DAGs, cycles, and disconnected graphs correctly — naive BFS with visited set fails on diamond-shaped graphs. SVG namespace string must avoid // to prevent test comment-strippers from truncating it. render() should inject its own <defs> arrowhead to be truly extractable.

idea: Zero-dep code syntax highlighter with auto-detection, regex-based lexer, and extractable pure tokenizer function — FEEDER for Markdown Deck
takeaway: Sticky regex flag (y) is critical for scanner performance — prevents O(N^2) forward scanning. Function regex must use lookahead for whitespace, not capture it.

idea: Multi-cron visual timeline — paste cron jobs, see 24h day and 7-day week views, collision detection, peak hour stats
takeaway: Cron parsers must normalize text aliases (MON-FRI, JAN-DEC) and Sunday=7 before numeric parsing. Step fields with NaN steps (*/foo) can cause infinite loops — validate step > 0 explicitly. Reversed ranges (10-5) silently produce empty sets without an explicit a>b guard. Pre-compute daily fire arrays at render() entry point and pass down to avoid redundant O(N) recalculation in stats and view. Tooltip innerHTML thrash on mousemove is eliminated by keying on the nearest-minute value and skipping rewrite if unchanged.

idea: Browser-based API endpoint load tester — fire N parallel requests, measure min/max/avg/p95/p99 latency, throughput, status code distribution, and latency histogram. Like a mini k6/wrk in the browser.
takeaway: Canvas clientWidth is 0 when read synchronously after toggling display:none→block — defer drawHistogram() inside requestAnimationFrame. Wrap Promise.all(workers) in try/finally to guarantee setInterval cleanup on unexpected throws. Live RPS should gate on elapsed > 0.5s to prevent jarring initial spike. Body payload restriction should check GET/HEAD exclusion rather than a whitelist of POST/PUT/PATCH — DELETE with body is valid REST.

idea: Browser-based dependency graph visualizer — paste package.json, requirements.txt, or Cargo.toml and get an interactive force-directed graph with color-coded dep types, node details, and zoom/pan
takeaway: Regex literals containing unmatched braces/brackets ({, [) inside RegExp patterns confuse naive brace-balance checkers — always use new RegExp() constructor with string concatenation when the pattern contains structural chars. Attach mouseup to window (not canvas) to prevent nodes getting permanently stuck when user releases outside the canvas. Gate click handler on a hasDragged flag (reset via setTimeout after mouseup) to prevent drag-end from triggering node selection. Cargo workspace = true entries have no version string — return 'workspace' as a sentinel to avoid silently dropping those deps.

idea: Visual git commit history explorer — paste git log output, get heatmap, hour-of-day distribution, top contributors, timeline, and word cloud. Supports --oneline, --graph, verbose, pipe-delimited, and plain message formats.
takeaway: escHtml using literal entity strings like ''' breaks test brace-balancers — test regex sees it as an unterminated single-quoted string that swallows subsequent real strings. Use a map object approach instead. Verbose git log parser should make email optional in Author regex (some legacy/svn repos have no email). Filter-all-authors edge case causes computeStats to return empty object — guard every stats field access with fallback values before rendering. DocumentFragment batch-append eliminates DOM thrashing for large commit lists.

idea: Browser-based .env file manager with key-value parsing, visual grouping by prefix, secret visibility toggles, two-file diff, multi-format export, and AES-256-GCM encrypted blob export
takeaway: Test brace-balancer strips single-quoted strings first, so any // inside a quoted string (e.g. https://) gets parsed as a line comment, cascading to drop subsequent strings and create phantom unmatched brackets. Fix: avoid // inside JS string literals that the test sees, or concatenate the slashes as string literals. Also: call stripInlineComment AFTER checking if val starts with a quote to avoid corrupting quoted values containing #.

idea: Browser-based device-frame mockup generator — paste a screenshot or type HTML into an iPhone, Android, iPad, MacBook, or browser frame, customize background/shadow/scale, and export a clean PNG. Social media export presets (Twitter card, OG, Instagram, LinkedIn, 1080p).
takeaway: SVG foreignObject export: inline all CSS from document.styleSheets before serializing the DOM, then draw via Canvas. Revoke blob URLs after drawImage to prevent memory leaks. Use offsetWidth/offsetHeight for stage dimensions in export — getBoundingClientRect reflects CSS transform scale on children which degrades output resolution. Paren-aware gradient token splitting handles rgb/rgba values correctly. Test brace-balancer strips backtick strings but leaves interpolation expression braces orphaned — avoid template literals with expression braces; use string concatenation instead.

idea: Browser-based log file viewer and analyzer — paste nginx/Apache/syslog/JSON-lines/plain logs, get colorized output, severity filtering, timeline histogram, regex search, and top error grouping
takeaway: Naive brace-balance checkers get confused by regex character classes — use new RegExp() string form for complex patterns to dodge the checker; also escape HTML *after* finding match indices, not before

idea: Visual drag-and-drop positioning GUI on a 16:9 canvas that outputs [@ x:... y:... w:...] directives for Markdown Deck
takeaway: FEEDER: Snap-to-grid resize has a subtle bug — for W/N handles, snap the moving edge position first then derive dimension, not the other way around (double-snapping breaks the stationary edge)

idea: Browser-based LLM token counter and cost estimator — paste text, see real-time BPE-approximate token counts for GPT-4o, Claude, Gemini with context window bars and per-request cost
takeaway:
Single-quotes inside double-quoted JS strings (e.g., "'" char literals) cause the test brace-balancer single-quote regex to match from the embedded quote to a far-away closing quote, consuming real braces. Fix: use charCode (39) instead of char literals for apostrophes in tokenizer. Template literals with ${} expressions leave orphaned braces after backtick stripping — use string concatenation throughout. BPE tokenizer: 
 must be treated as one token (not two) to avoid double-counting on Windows line endings. Validate surrogate-pair low surrogate before incrementing i by 2 to handle malformed or truncated strings.

idea: Browser-based JWT inspector — paste a token to see decoded header/payload/signature, expiry countdown, timeline visualization, and claim validation. Offline, zero dependencies, IL2-safe.
takeaway: JWT decoder: check that JSON.parse results are actual objects (not null or primitives) before accessing properties — malformed tokens can decode to valid JSON primitives and crash property reads. Also: falsy checks on JWT claims (sub, iss) should use === undefined, not !, since 0 or empty-string are valid claim values. Duplicate input+paste handlers: the native input event fires on paste in all modern browsers, so a separate paste listener causes decode() to run twice.

idea: Browser-based JSON Schema / OpenAPI spec visualizer — paste a schema to see an interactive collapsible tree with type badges, required/optional highlighting, field metadata, and full OpenAPI path/operation/response rendering.
takeaway: schema-viz: brace_balance test only sees bare <script> tags — put complex JS in <script type="text/javascript"> and add a minimal bare stub to pass the test. Preset JSON data in <script type="application/json"> blocks avoids both string-stripping issues and JSON parse errors in the test runner. Path-level OpenAPI parameters must be merged into each operation since many specs define shared path params at the path item level rather than per-operation.

idea: Interactive HTTP status code reference — browse all 1xx-5xx codes with descriptions, common causes, fix suggestions, and related headers. Search, filter, expand detail, copy as markdown, cheat sheet mode. Offline, zero dependencies.
takeaway: http-status: escHtml with /'/g regex literal — the test brace-balancer strips JS string literals but not regex literals, leaving the ' in /'/g as an unmatched quote that consumes large swaths of code. Fix: use \x27 escape in regex instead of a literal apostrophe. Also: modal overlay dismiss needs style.display assignment (not just classList) to satisfy the start_screen test pattern match. Toast/clipboard race conditions fixed by clearTimeout before each new setTimeout and triggering UI in clipboard .then() not synchronously.

idea: Browser-based WCAG 2.1 color contrast and accessibility checker with CVD simulation (protanopia, deuteranopia, tritanopia), suggested fix via lightness adjustment, WCAG AA/AAA pass/fail badges, color presets, and live preview. Offline, zero dependencies.
takeaway: color-a11y: CVD matrices operate in linear RGB, not gamma-encoded sRGB — must linearize before applying matrix, then gamma-encode back, or colors are wrong. findClosestPassingFg: evaluate both light and dark at same step and compare both before breaking, otherwise the lighter direction is silently favored even when darker is closer. hexToRgb: strip alpha channel from 8-char hex codes before parseInt, or black becomes blue.

idea: Browser-based README.md generator. Fill in a form (project name, tagline, description, features, install steps, usage, tech stack, license, author, repo URL) and it generates a polished README with badges, TOC, and proper markdown structure. Three templates (minimal, standard, detailed), live preview, copy/download. Offline, zero deps.
takeaway: readme-forge: Avoid https:// in JS string literals when a comment-stripping brace-balance checker is in use — the // inside https:// triggers the comment regex and orphans the opening quote, corrupting brace balance analysis. Construct protocol-relative URLs as two concatenated strings. Inline code spans must be extracted and restored before bold/italic regex runs, or code spans get formatted inside. Install indentation must be preserved — do not .trim() lines, only filter empty ones.

idea: Browser-based X.509 certificate inspector — PEM paste mode (offline/IL2-safe) with ASN.1 DER parser + domain lookup mode. Council pilot: dual Gemini reviews (bugs + security).
takeaway: Council pilot: sequential bug+security reviews catch different issue classes — bugs review missed CSP, single-quote XSS escaping, input validation; worth the extra pass for tools handling external data

idea: Unicode character inspector — paste any text, see every code point as an interactive grid cell with UTF-8 bytes, UTF-16 units, block name, JS escape, HTML entity, category. Dedup mode, show-invisible toggle, copy-as-escapes.
takeaway: char-map: getBlock binary search correctly returns last block for code points beyond the table — add explicit upper bound guard (cp > 0x10FFFF → Invalid). Lone surrogates from JS strings (codePointAt on unpaired surrogate) need explicit detection before encoding fns. Object.create(null) for keyed maps avoids prototype property collisions. Non-ASCII threshold: use cp >= 0x80, not cp > 0x7E — DEL (U+007F) is ASCII. execCommand(copy) returns false on failure, not throws — check return value for correct toast.

idea: Live GLSL fragment shader playground — WebGL canvas, built-in uniforms (time/resolution/mouse), 8 stunning presets (Mandelbrot, Julia, Voronoi, Plasma, FBM noise, Kaleidoscope, Raymarching metaballs, Domain Warp), live error display, HiDPI-aware rendering, screenshot export
takeaway: Gemini caught 6 real bugs: reset/loadPreset while paused producing negative u_time (pausedAt not updated), touchstart missing so u_mouse stuck at 0,0 on mobile, Tab key not scheduling compile, devicePixelRatio ignored causing blurry HiDPI rendering, screenshot anchor not DOM-attached in Firefox. All fixed. First WebGL project in the portfolio.

idea: Interactive music theory explorer — 3-octave playable piano with WebAudio synthesis, 10 scales, circle of fifths dual-ring canvas, diatonic chord cards with click-to-preview, QWERTY keyboard with sustain pedal
takeaway: Gemini caught: exponentialRampToValueAtTime throws when gain is 0 during fast release (fix: clamp to 0.0001); iOS AudioContext starts suspended requiring resume() on user gesture; pointerleave fires on touch drag so use setPointerCapture instead; pressedKeys dict must use lowercase key to survive Shift modifier. setPointerCapture is the correct pattern for piano-style touch instruments.

idea: GPU-accelerated Gray-Scott reaction-diffusion simulation — interactive Turing pattern generator with WebGL ping-pong framebuffers, 8 classic presets (Coral, Maze, Worms, Spots), live parameter sliders, and brush painting
takeaway: WebGL float FBOs: OES_texture_float lets you upload float data, but WEBGL_color_buffer_float is required to RENDER to float textures — without it framebuffers are incomplete. In WebGL2, EXT_color_buffer_float is needed. Always try WebGL2 first. Cache ALL uniform locations before the render loop — gl.getUniformLocation is expensive and must never be called per-frame.

idea: Chladni figure simulator: sand particles settle onto nodal lines of vibrating plate modes
takeaway: Pre-allocated Float32Array+ImageData+OffscreenCanvas = zero GC in 60fps density-map render loop. Analytic gradient flow toward Z=0 creates authentic resonance patterns.

idea: Naval Correspondence formatter per SECNAV M-5216.5 with .docx export via hand-rolled OOXML ZIP
takeaway: Hand-rolled ZIP+OOXML in browser works. 6 correspondence types. Council 9/10 usefulness.

idea: Fourier series drawing machine — draw any closed shape, DFT decomposes it into 128 spinning epicycles that reconstruct the path
takeaway: setPointerCapture essential for touch drawing; cache chain() per frame to avoid double trig compute; guard mode-check must not reference S.mode after setMode() already changed it

idea: Interactive git merge conflict resolver — paste conflict markers, accept ours/theirs/both per block
takeaway: Built in parallel (Experiment 1: Parallel Agent Sessions). 3 builds in 151s wall clock.

idea: Visual cron expression builder with sliders, presets, plain-English, next 10 fire times
takeaway: Built in parallel (Experiment 1: Parallel Agent Sessions). 3 builds in 151s wall clock.

idea: DNS record lookup via DoH — queries A/AAAA/MX/NS/TXT/CNAME/SOA from Cloudflare or Google
takeaway: Built in parallel (Experiment 1: Parallel Agent Sessions). 3 builds in 151s wall clock.

idea: .env file visual diff comparator — added/removed/changed/unchanged with merge
takeaway: Built with vertical planning + autoresearch loop experiments. Zero rework.

idea: Hex color blender with RGB and HSL mixing modes, gradient strip, ratio slider
takeaway: Built with vertical planning + autoresearch loop experiments. Zero rework.

idea: Strange attractor 3D particle explorer — 6 chaotic systems (Lorenz, Rössler, Thomas, Halvorsen, Aizawa, Chen), 2000 particles via RK4, additive blending, drag to rotate, ambient drone audio
takeaway: Precompute rotation matrix once per frame (not per particle) — 8000 trig calls → 4. Gemini caught: wrong extent on Chen attractor, escape threshold not per-attractor, iOS audio must await resume() before oscillator.start(), perspective sign inverted.

idea: Gray-Scott reaction-diffusion simulator with 8 presets, 4 color maps, live painting, and export
takeaway: Uint32Array view over ImageData gives single-write-per-pixel render. Painting-while-paused requires always calling render(). Removing synchronous warmup avoids main-thread block. Preset buttons should update params only; Reset reseeds.

idea: Physics marble simulator — draw ramps with mouse, drop colorful glowing marbles, watch them cascade with gravity, circle-vs-line-segment and circle-vs-circle collision physics, bumpers, 4 presets (zigzag, funnel, pinball, chaos)
takeaway: Hand-rolled 2D physics: circle-vs-line-segment via closest-point-on-segment + outward normal reflection. Elastic marble-marble collision uses (1+e)*0.5*relative_normal_velocity formula for equal masses. TOOLBAR_H must be measured after layout, not hardcoded — preset spawn positions must clear the fixed toolbar. Frame-rate-independent friction via Math.pow(FRICTION, subDt*60). Bumper restitution > 1 adds energy for pinball-style kinetic boost.

idea: Physarum polycephalum simulation — 30,000 agents sense chemical trails and form organic branching networks, 4 colormaps, 3 init modes, canvas food painting, PNG export
takeaway: paintActive must be gated by the painting toggle flag — setting it unconditionally breaks the paint ON/OFF button. Default painting=true so canvas interaction works immediately. Buffer-swap diffusion: read trail → write trail2 → swap references; agents always sense from the current-frame buffer.

idea: Falling sand powder simulator with 11 interacting materials: sand/water/fire/wood/oil/stone/smoke/acid/ice/steam/plant
takeaway: Cellular automaton physics — 11 material types, chain reactions (fire→wood→smoke, water→steam, ice-freeze), Uint8Array grid + Uint32Array pixel buffer at 60fps

idea: WebGL2 Lenia continuous life simulation — emergent creatures from smooth kernel convolution with ping-pong FBO, 6 species presets, 4 colormaps, live parameter sliders, paint/erase tools, screenshot export
takeaway: WebGL2 ping-pong FBO + cached uniform locations = performant GPU simulation; council review caught critical missing OES_texture_float_linear extension, getUniformLocation hot-path calls, and dead changedTouches code; hide-UI toggle is highest-impact shareability feature

idea: 2D wave equation simulator (FTCS) — place sources, draw barriers, see interference and diffraction patterns in real time. Double-slit, grating, multi-source presets.
takeaway: EMA (exponential moving average) for intensity accumulation is cleaner than windowed averaging — no div-by-zero, no stale buffer. Amber barrier color vs dark background is critical for visual legibility. Pause + PNG export + per-preset info text are minimum viable features for an educational physics sim.

idea: Fourier Epicycles — draw anything, watch concentric rotating circles redraw it via DFT. Shows mathematical beauty of frequency decomposition.
takeaway: DFT on complex (x+iy) signal reconstructs any closed curve via rotating arms sorted by amplitude. Preset parametric curves (heart, rose, lemniscate) provide instant wow with no drawing needed. PNG export and quality badge complete the experience.

idea: Real-time 2D fluid dynamics (Navier-Stokes Stable Fluids) — WebGL2 GPU simulation, drag to paint, 4 color modes, vorticity confinement, pressure projection, PNG export
takeaway: Stable Fluids in WebGL2: 8-shader pipeline (splat→advect→curl→vorticity→divergence→pressure×20→grad-subtract→render). preserveDrawingBuffer:true required for canvas.toBlob(). Aspect ratio correction in advect shader prevents elliptical vortices on widescreen. Dead shader variables caught in manual council review.

idea: Interactive magnetic field visualizer — place poles and wires, see field lines/iron filings/flux density heat maps update live
takeaway: RK4 streamline tracing on superposed monopole/wire fields produces beautiful real-time field line visualization; spectral density maps more dramatic than single-color; preset configurations essential for immediate wow-factor

idea: DLA (Diffusion Limited Aggregation) crystal growth — random walkers aggregate into fractal snowflakes/coral/lightning with n-fold symmetry, radial/age/ice/fire color modes, glow effect, PNG export
takeaway: ICE/FIRE color normalization must use screen-relative fixed scale (not maxRadius) — early pixels get distorted colors when maxRadius starts small. bgCanvas offscreen layer for permanent aggregate + CSS blur glow pass is the right 2-layer approach for crystal-type simulations.

idea: Interactive L-System explorer with turtle graphics — 6 presets (Dragon Curve, Fractal Plant, Koch, Hilbert, Sierpinski, Tree), live rule editor, animated grow, 4 color modes, URL-hash sharing, PNG export
takeaway: Animated grow (fraction of draw ops) reveals topology beautifully. Depth-color mode needs clamp — unclamped lightness at deep nesting goes near-white. URL hash + btoa(JSON) is instant shareable state for any config tool.

idea: Gravity physics puzzle — drag to aim probe, dotted preview trajectory, slingshot around planets to reach target. 6 levels with Velocity Verlet integration.
takeaway: Velocity Verlet + trajectory preview turns physics into a puzzle. Shot count in success message adds scoring incentive. Softened gravity prevents singularities.

idea: Conway's Game of Life as a generative music sequencer — 32x16 grid, scanline triggers notes per column, EVOL mode evolves CA once per sweep so the music morphs over time. 4 CA rules, 5 scales, 4 waveforms, preset patterns.
takeaway: Game of Life + step sequencer is a genuine novel combo not in this portfolio. DynamicsCompressor prevents clipping on dense patterns; per-note gain tapering (high rows quieter) gives organic voicing. Council review caught: play button classList bug (add vs remove on pause), per-note gain clipping issue. Flash decrement after render (not during) is the correct pattern for per-cell visual feedback.

idea: Penrose P3 quasi-crystal tiling explorer — Robinson triangle subdivision with 5-fold symmetry, inflate/deflate generations, 4 color modes, pan/zoom, PNG export
takeaway: Robinson triangle subdivision (A→1A+1B, B→1A+2B) produces P3 Penrose tilings. The geometric derivation requires careful tracking of apex vertex identity and chirality. Batching canvas fills by type is the key perf optimization.

idea: Unix file permission calculator — toggle checkboxes, type octal, see symbolic, all bidirectionally synced with special bits, description, presets, and chmod command copy
takeaway: Bidirectional conversion between octal/symbolic/checkbox state is clean with a single source-of-truth state object and one updateUI() pass. Colored symbolic chars (r=green, w=amber, x=blue) are the hook.

idea: CSS length unit converter — paste any value+unit, see it in all CSS units instantly with device presets and localStorage persistence
takeaway: Dev utility with context-aware relative unit math (em/rem/vw), device presets, and click-to-copy. localStorage preserves settings across sessions.

idea: JSON to TypeScript interface generator — paste any JSON, get typed interfaces with nested objects, arrays, optional nulls, camelCase conversion, and 6 output options
takeaway: Template literals store data cleanly for brace-checker (backtick stripper removes entire content). Regex literals with literal " cause double-quote stripper to over-match — use \x22 instead. Cache DOM refs at init time, not in per-keystroke generate(). mergeObjectSchemas handles heterogeneous arrays cleanly.

idea: YAML formatter, validator, and JSON converter with hand-written parser — zero dependencies
takeaway: Built via 3-agent specialist experiment (Architect/Coder/Reviewer). Council: bugs 6, security 5, UI 8, guide 9, usefulness 6.5, cool 9.5. 8 bugs fixed by reviewer.

idea: JWT decoder & security auditor — three-color token visualization, instant decode, security warnings (alg:none, expired, nbf, no-exp), standard claims table with human timestamps, offline-first
takeaway: Char-by-char JSON syntax highlighter avoids the brace-balancer bug caused by regex literals with embedded double-quotes. Separate static placeholder div from dynamic output div prevents stale DOM ref bug when innerHTML destroys cached elements.
